paper: VeriEvol: Scaling Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning via Verifiable Evol-Instruct

research_equation.original_problem: Scaling multimodal mathematical reasoning requires more than harder questions; labels must remain reliable as data volume grows, but existing methods either trust the labeller (data-pipeline family) or assume answers are correct (policy-side family).
research_equation.reconstructed_problem: Multimodal RL data construction is a verifiable two-axis optimization problem: maximize prompt difficulty (via route-specific evolution) AND maximize answer reliability (via offline hypothesis-test falsification), with both axes decoupled from any policy update.
research_equation.research_equation: VeriEvol: argmax_{O_z, v, g} E_{benchmarks}[Acc(GRPO(D_SFT + D_RL[O_z(q), v(â̂), g]))] s.t. prompt difficulty ⊥ answer reliability
research_equation.key_insight: Decoupling two axes before policy update makes scaling properties near-additive, independently auditable, and directly compatible with existing GRPO recipes.

pass_1_first_principles.what_is_essential: Data construction for visual-math RL → two fundamental requirements: (1) prompts must be hard and image-grounded, (2) answers must be reliable enough to serve as repeated reward signals.
pass_1_first_principles.what_is_incidental: The specific 12-topic taxonomy, the choice of Qwen2.5-VL backbone, the exact GRPO hyperparameters — these are implementation choices, not first-principle requirements.
first_principles_decomposition: P1: Seed prompts are too easy → need controlled difficulty amplification with image grounding guarantee
P2: Answers must survive adversarial refutation before becoming reward → offline verification decoupled from policy
P3: Different visual domains need different difficulty operators → routing by problem family
P4: Single-channel verification misses errors → multi-channel complementary evidence (code + visual)
P5: Scaling must be monotonic and auditable → component separation with traceable metadata

pass_2_bridge_to_paper.p1_mapping: Type-aware evolution module (Section 3.2): routing → route-specific operators → question gate φ_q with 5 conjunctive checks
pass_2_bridge_to_paper.p2_mapping: HTV-Agent (Section 3.3): 4-stage pipeline (solvers → verifiers → decider → gate) with refutation-seeking framing
pass_2_bridge_to_paper.p3_mapping: 12-topic routing taxonomy + route-specific operator sets O_z (Appendix C)
pass_2_bridge_to_paper.p4_mapping: Code-checking module (Python AST assertions) + visual-evidence module (OCR, crops, pixel probes) as distinct channels
pass_2_bridge_to_paper.p5_mapping: Separate D_SFT / D_RL materialization + per-sample verifier trace + rollout-based curriculum filtering

what_is_well_supported: SFT and RL data scaling both monotonic across ~1 order of magnitude
Near-additive RL contributions confirming orthogonal axes
Verifier channel complementarity demonstrated via inference-time ablation
what_could_be_stronger: Verifier-on/off ablation not directly matched at RL budget (inference-time proxy only)
Single seed per RL run — statistical significance of per-benchmark deltas not established
60-sample single-annotator pilot for error analysis — qualitative only
SFT init interaction with RL data construction acknowledged but not controlled
External baselines from original papers (not re-run) — different harnesses
what_remains_open: Full cross-product composition of (route × skill × answer-type × verifier-contract) beyond 12-topic discretization
Heterogeneous verifier ensembles (different backbones, tool stacks)
Generalization beyond mathematical reasoning to chart QA, science diagrams, document understanding
Backbone-capacity ceiling at larger model scales (beyond 7B)
Effectiveness with synthetic image generators vs natural image seeds
pass_3_gap_analysis.critical_evaluation: The paper's core claims are well-supported by clean ablations and monotonic scaling curves. The strongest evidence is the controlled RL ablation where only prompt evolution and verifier are swapped while SFT init and GRPO recipe remain fixed — this is methodologically sound. The main weakness is the indirect verifier isolation (inference-time proxy rather than matched RL budget), which the authors explicitly acknowledge. The error analysis is appropriately cautious (n=60, single annotator). Overall, this is a rigorous empirical paper with honest limitations.

challenge_1_prompt_difficulty.description: Seeds are low-difficulty recognition/lookup; need to amplify reasoning difficulty while keeping image grounding necessary.
modules: Type-aware routing: z = (t, y, u, c) schema-constrained classifier
Route-specific operators O_z: constrained prompt templates per 12-topic taxonomy
Question-level gate φ_q: 5 conjunctive checks (img, cons, vis, hard, dedup)
challenge_1_prompt_difficulty.design_rationale: Routing prevents operator mismatch (geometry template on charts). Independent gate prevents generator self-reward bias.

challenge_2_answer_reliability.description: Answers must survive as reward signals across thousands of rollouts; a single wrong label is reinforced systematically.
modules: Refutation-seeking verifiers: code-checking (Python AST) + visual-evidence (OCR, probes)
Conflict-aware decider: constrained LLM, no tool use, assesses verifier quality
Deterministic acceptance gate: g(â,m) = 𝟙[G_schema ∧ G_evidence ∧ G_verify ∧ G_program ∧ G_consensus]
challenge_2_answer_reliability.design_rationale: Multi-solver exposes disagreement; refutation framing probes VG/TS failure modes; conjunctive gate is stricter than majority voting.

challenge_3_training_signal_materializat.description: Verified samples must be usable by existing SFT and GRPO recipes without modification.
modules: D_SFT materialization: (I, q', r, â, e) — retains trace
D_RL materialization: (I, q', â, v, m) — trace removed, deterministic checker
Rollout-based curriculum: ρ(x) estimation → intermediate ρ prioritized
challenge_3_training_signal_materializat.design_rationale: Standard prompt-answer-reward tuple format ensures plug-and-play compatibility.

challenge_4_scaling_without_degradation.description: Data construction must remain useful across orders of magnitude; verification must not introduce systematic bias.
modules: Separate evolution (O_z) and verification (v, g) so each scales independently
Rollout pass rate ρ filtering removes trivially solved (ρ=1) and intractable (ρ=0) samples
challenge_4_scaling_without_degradation.design_rationale: Component separation avoids single end-to-end generator where all interfaces fail together.

challenge_5_verifier_modularity.description: New verification strategies must be addable without redesigning the policy optimizer.
modules: Plugin architecture: evidence channels (code-checking, visual-evidence) are independent modules
Decider consumes all channel outputs; doesn't assume verifier > solver
Gate is conjunctive — any new channel adds a new conjunctive term
challenge_5_verifier_modularity.design_rationale: Evidence channels target different failure modes; complementarity empirically confirmed.
